summary: Complete multi-tenant data isolation testing passed. All 16 tests verified that tenant_id filtering is working correctly. Tenant nyla-air-water sees 68 leads, 23 users, 6 accounts, 29 tasks. Tenant acme-corp sees 0 leads, 1 user, 0 accounts, 0 tasks. Cross-tenant access is properly blocked (401 for login, 404 for resources).
passed_tests: Cross-tenant login rejected - admin@nylaairwater.earth cannot login with acme-corp tenant header (401)
Leads count difference verified between tenants
Users count difference verified between tenants
Activities API - Tenant 1 can access activities for their leads
Cross-data access prevention - Tenant 2 cannot access Tenant 1's specific lead (404)
Data isolation summary - All resources properly isolated
critical_code_review_comments: TenantCollection and TenantDB classes correctly implement automatic tenant_id filtering
GLOBAL_COLLECTIONS (tenants, user_sessions, master_skus, etc.) are correctly excluded from tenant filtering
Auth routes properly use get_tdb() for tenant-aware user lookup
Login creates sessions with tenant_id for future reference
seed_data_creation: No seed data created - using existing tenant data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-tenant data isolation is fully working. TenantDB wrapper automatically adds tenant_id to all queries for tenant-specific collections. X-Tenant-ID header must be included in all API requests. Cross-tenant access is properly blocked with 401 for auth and 404 for specific resources. Global collections like master_skus, tenants, user_sessions are not filtered by tenant.
